title: runStudy broken: TV WS rejects STD; and Script$PUB; wire IDs with 'check study unexpected error'
description:
TradingView's prodata.tradingview.com WebSocket now rejects every STD;*@tv-basicstudies-*! and Script\$PUB;*@tv-scripting-101! create_study dispatch with study_error param[3] = "check study unexpected error". This breaks the worker's primary code paths (worker/src/tradingview.ts:runStudy and worker/src/chart-session-do.ts:handleStudyCreate).

Reproduction: scripts/probes/-style runners under /tmp/tv-aes-probe/ exercise create_study after series_completed using both authed and anon tokens, both endpoints (data, prodata), every input shape (empty, in_0/in_1, full metaInfo defaults including the AES-encrypted text blob), every turnaround value, and every reasonable wireId variation. Every STD; and PUB; permutation returns the same error within ~50ms of dispatch. Series streaming continues normally afterward, so the connection itself is healthy.

What still works:
  - RSI@tv-basicstudies-45! (legacy bare id + integer version)
  - Volume@tv-basicstudies-118!, Volume@tv-basicstudies-241!
  - PivotPointsHighLow@tv-basicstudies-241!

What returns "Study not allowed in this connection" (entitlement gate, not protocol error):
  - MACD, BB, Stochastic, ATR, VWAP (legacy bare ids on basicstudies-241)

What breaks the same way as STD;:
  - Mode B inline-IL execution (text/pineId/pineVersion/pineFeatures envelope) → "Study not allowed in this connection"

Hypothesis: TradingView migrated STD;-prefixed studies and public Pine scripts to a non-WS execution path (probably an HTTP study-runner endpoint we haven't discovered) and left the legacy JAR-bundled basicstudies on the WS create_study verb. The pine-facade /versions and /translate endpoints still serve metaInfo for STD; ids, which is misleading.

Impact:
  - /v1/study returns "study_error: check study unexpected error" for every STD; or PUB; studyId against a real session.
  - /v1/study/chain (modify_study fan-out) inherits the same failure.
  - Chart Session DO /create followed by /study fails identically.
  - Tier 1 Algorganic capture (PUB;edfaff05350f406092874780e934f06c) is blocked at protocol level.
  - Existing tests (worker/src/index.test.ts, worker/src/study-chain.test.ts) all mock connect/runStudy so the regression is invisible to CI.

Next steps:
  - Capture a real browser session against tradingview.com (DevTools → Network → WS) and diff against our wire frames to find the missing setup verb / new endpoint.
  - Until resolved, treat /v1/study, /v1/study/chain, and the chart-session study verbs as known-broken for STD;/PUB; ids.
  - Probe corpus lives in /tmp/tv-aes-probe/ (not committed; transient evidence). Key runners: study-matrix.mjs, wireid-namespace-matrix.mjs, legacy-id-matrix.mjs, pub-script-matrix.mjs, pub-prefix-matrix.mjs, quote-flow-probe.mjs, rsi-full-input-probe.mjs.
notes: LIVE PROBE CONFIRMS UPSTREAM 2026-05-07 11:11Z (worker version 3200bcfc): the wire-form rewrite landed in worker/src/tradingview.ts + chart-session-do.ts (resolveStudyWireId drop STD;, basicstudies-265, Script@tv-scripting-101!, pineId injection) was deployed and exercised against fresh authed session (sessionid=lll9z24ama221cqefl3zpvhe0k56tlw9). TV WS still rejects every STD;/PUB;-derived create_study with 'check study unexpected error' within ~50ms even with the corrected wire form. This corroborates the bead's hypothesis that TV migrated STD;/PUB; execution off the WS verb to an HTTP study-runner endpoint we have not yet discovered. Status promoted to verification_failed_root_cause_upstream — local fixes are inert against this upstream change. Investigation must capture a live browser session against tradingview.com (DevTools Network tab) to identify the new HTTP study-runner endpoint or alternate WS verb.

design:
Resolved design decisions surfaced from reading worker/src/strategy.ts:30-46, worker/src/tradingview.ts:968-1003, skills/tradingview/reference/strategies.md:17/91-94/147 before any code lands.

(D1) New normalizer signature in worker/src/strategy.ts:

  Replaces buildStrategyInputs (worker/src/strategy.ts:146-164). Old function removed (no shim — internal API only).

(D2) Wire form: in_0 is a STRUCTURED DICT bundling all 16 strategy properties + the user input that would have lived at slot 0. Subsequent user inputs (in_1, in_2, ...) stay at top-level of the inputs dict. Diagnostics include wireForm: conservative-bundle so consumers know assumption. (Open question O1 in notes.)

(D4) StudyRequest gains inputsPreShaped?: boolean. runStudy reads this and bypasses buildInputsDict params/type-rewrite logic when set, trusting caller to have produced wire-form inputs (with source/symbol rewrites already applied inside the in_0 dict where applicable).

(D5) runStrategy fetches getIndicatorMeta itself (KV-cached, double-fetch acceptable), calls buildStrategyWireInputs which does its own source-typed and symbol-typed rewrites internally on the in_0 sub-dict, then calls runStudy with inputsPreShaped: true.

(D6) Diagnostics record shape (returned in StrategyResult.wireDiagnostics):
  { acceptedProperties: string[], rejectedProperties: Record<string, any>, enumViolations: Array<{key, value, allowed}>, inputCollisions: Array<{key, propertyValue, inputValue}>, sourceRewrites: Array<{id, before, after}>, symbolRewrites: Array<{id, before, after}>, paramAliases: Array<{name, resolvedId}>, wireForm: "conservative-bundle" }

(D7) packages/tradingview-core/src/strategy.ts (new file):

  - export const STRATEGY_DEFAULT_QTY_TYPES: ReadonlySet<string> — {fixed, cash_per_order, percent_of_equity}

(D8) scripts/validate-tradingview-skill.mjs extension: assert STRATEGY_PROPERTY_KEYS, STRATEGY_DEFAULT_QTY_TYPES, STRATEGY_COMMISSION_TYPES, isStudyStrategy, isStudyStrategyStub appear as exports in core. Keeps skill/core in sync.

Uses synthetic metaInfo fixture committed to the repo.

(D10) parseStrategyOutputs (worker/src/strategy.ts:319-381) and the REPORT_FIELD_ALIASES table stay unchanged in this slice — out of scope. Also out of scope: report field renames to skill snake_case literals (deferred to a follow-up bead since it changes the public response shape).

(D11) The metric ranking default in StrategyOptimizeRequest (worker/src/strategy.ts:123) currently defaults to netProfit. Slice A item 1 does NOT change this default (Sortino/Sharpe defaulting belongs to Slice C analyze suite per bd memory always-use-sortino-over-sharpe-in-metric-defaults).

title: Slice F: Local-watchlist real-time streaming (arbitrary symbol list -> SSE)
description:
Client supplies an arbitrary symbol list (stored locally, not in TV cloud) and receives streamed quote updates and minute-bar closes. Extends StreamBridge DO; minimal coupling to Slices A-D.

Use cases:
- A fund's portfolio of 50 tickers with live LTP/bid/ask + minute-bar OHLC.
- A model-driven candidate list refreshed daily, streamed for the trading session.
- Multi-asset multi-exchange dashboard with up to N symbols (N capped per safety gates below).

Endpoints:
- POST /v1/quotes/stream/subscribe { symbols, fields?, includeMinuteBars? } → { streamId }
- GET  /v1/quotes/stream/{id}/sse — SSE: event:quote (qsd-derived), event:bar (minute close from chart series), event:error, Last-Event-ID resume
- POST /v1/quotes/stream/{id}/update { add?, remove? }
- POST /v1/quotes/stream/{id}/close

Wire (per quote-session pattern):
1. Worker opens TV WS via /v1/stream/bootstrap (data.tradingview.com) — admin session required.
2. quote_create_session [qs1]
3. quote_set_fields [qs1, ...fields]
4. quote_add_symbols [qs1, ...symbols]
5. Receive qsd frames; debounce + forward to client SSE.
6. For minute bars: same WS, chart_create_session + create_series at tf=1; parse timescale_update / du.
7. Client reconnect: Last-Event-ID restores cursor in StreamBridge ring buffer; quote subscriptions reattach idempotently.

Safety / resource gates (NEW):
- MAX_SYMBOLS_PER_STREAM = 100 (configurable via env, default 100); subscribe with > limit returns 400 with limit-spec.
- MAX_STREAMS_PER_HMAC_CLIENT = 5 concurrent; sixth subscribe returns 429 quota-exceeded.
- IDLE_STREAM_AUTO_CLOSE = 5 min after last SSE consumer disconnect.
- TV_WS_BACKOFF: exponential 1s/2s/4s/8s/16s on TV WS open failure; surrender to client as event:error after 5 retries.
- TV session failure: if TV returns auth_error on WS open, mark stored session blocked (existing markAuthFailure flow); return 401 to client; do not retry indefinitely.
- Throttle: max one event:quote per (symbol, field) per 250ms forwarded to client.
- Cleanup: client SSE close → mark consumer gone; if 0 consumers + idle > IDLE_STREAM_AUTO_CLOSE → close TV WS, evict ring buffer, drop DO storage row.
- HMAC required on subscribe / update / close; sse may opt to require HMAC on initial GET if browser EventSource (header-less) is not supported (TBD per existing /v1/stream/* posture).

Lifecycle ownership:
- StreamBridge DO extension (preferred) since it already has SSE + ring buffer + Last-Event-ID; OR new QuoteStream DO if migration cost is favorable. Decision recorded in PR.
acceptance_criteria: Probe 1 — subscribe 10 symbols, receive ≥1 event:quote each within 5s. Probe 2 — includeMinuteBars:true on 5 symbols; observe one event:bar per symbol per minute boundary across a 3-minute window. Probe 3 — disconnect mid-stream, reconnect with Last-Event-ID, receive backfilled events from ring buffer. Probe 4 — update { add, remove } reshapes subscription without dropping unrelated streams. Probe 5 (NEW safety) — subscribe with 101 symbols → 400 with limit-spec; 6th concurrent stream from same HMAC client → 429; idle stream auto-closes 5 min after last consumer disconnects. Probe 6 — TV auth failure on WS open → 401 surface to client + stored session marked blocked; no retry storm. Probe 7 — quote throttle: emitting 100 qsd updates within 1s for one symbol field results in ≤4 SSE events forwarded. 5 local gates green.
notes:
Slice F locally complete: new QuoteStream DO + 5 endpoints (/v1/quotes/stream/{subscribe,:id/sse,:id/update,:id/close,active}) + KV-backed registry for per-HMAC quotas. Safety gates implemented and unit-tested:
- MAX_SYMBOLS_PER_STREAM=100 (init + /update both cap)
- MAX_STREAMS_PER_HMAC_CLIENT=5 (lazy GC on KV registry; 6th subscribe → 429)
- IDLE_STREAM_AUTO_CLOSE=5min via DO storage alarm
- 250ms per-symbol coalescing trailing-edge throttle (Probe 7: 100 qsd in 1s → 3-4 SSE events)
- TV_WS retry with bounded backoff [1s,2s,4s,8s,16s]; auth_error → 401 + admin-session blocked; no retry on auth-blocked.

Tests: worker/src/quote-stream-do.test.ts (20 cases), worker/src/quote-stream-registry.test.ts (6 cases). All 5 canonical gates green: typecheck, test (488 passing), build, skill:validate, openapi:validate.

OpenAPI: 5 new path entries added under /v1/quotes/stream/*. Skill reference: skills/tradingview/reference/endpoints.md updated with stream section.

Probe coverage:
- Probe 5 (safety gates): unit-tested ✓
- Probe 7 (throttle): unit-tested ✓
- Probes 1-4, 6 (live TV admin session, multi-minute boundary, mid-stream reconnect over wire, auth fail surfacing): operator-driven; deferred to live verification.

Implementation choice: separate QuoteStream DO rather than overloading StreamBridge — distinct upstream protocol (quote-session vs chart-only) and addressing model (KV registry of N concurrent streams per HMAC vs single bridge). End-State Bias preferred clean separation.

No breaking API changes: purely additive routes + new DO binding (migration v6 in wrangler.jsonc).

title: Slice E: Bulk OHLCV extractor (ohlcvExtract job type)
description:
Pull deep historical OHLCV across many symbols × many timeframes, persisted to R2 for offline analysis. Depends on Slice B (job DO).

Use cases:
- Entire history of one ticker across all timeframes in one shot.
- All US (or any country) common stocks at 1d for N years.
- All symbols matching a scanner filter (market cap > $1B, sector tech, exchange NYSE/NASDAQ) at requested TFs.

Inputs to /v1/jobs/submit { type: ohlcvExtract }:
- selector: { mode: 'symbols' | 'scanner', symbols?: string[], scannerFilter?: { market, columns, filter } }
- timeframes: ('1','5','15','60','240','1D','1W','1M')[]
- history: { fromTs?, toTs?, bars? } per timeframe
- options: { dataQuality, adjustment, sessionType, parallelism }

Storage contract (first implementation):
- Format: gzip-compressed JSON-Lines (.jsonl.gz), one bar per line, matching existing Worker compression patterns; deferring Parquet to follow-up unless a Worker-runtime-safe writer is selected.
- Path: backtest/jobs/<jobId>/ohlcv/<symbol>/<tf>.jsonl.gz
- Manifest: backtest/jobs/<jobId>/manifest.json with { jobId, cells, totalBars, missingCells[], errors[] }

Pipeline (Job DO orchestrates):
1. Resolve selector. If mode=scanner: call /v1/scan2 with paginated columns to enumerate matching symbols (handle 50k+ result sets via pagination).
2. Build cartesian (symbols × tfs).
3. For each cell: call existing /v1/candles + /v1/backfill (multi-window pagination) until history target reached or cap hit.
4. Persist per-cell result to gzip JSON-Lines under R2 path.
5. Emit manifest at the manifest path.
6. SSE progress: status / cell-complete (symbol, tf, bars) / done.

Parquet support is a separate follow-up bead, gated on selecting a Worker-safe Parquet writer (current candidates: parquet-wasm via Workers WASM, or a streamed columnar JSON-lines convention).

title: Slice D: CPCV with approxSlice + exactWindowed modes
description:
Combinatorial Purged Cross-Validation adapted to TradingView. Depends on Slice C.

Two named modes with different fidelity bars:

mode: approxSlice
- Single full-history run per param combo; slice equity[]/trades[] into N time blocks; compute fold metrics in-memory.
- Cost: |paramGrid| TV runs total.
- Cheap, but Pine var/varip persistent state, request.security warmup, position carry, and continuous-equity compounding all leak across 'folds.'
- Response MUST include contamination block: openPositionStraddleRate (% folds whose first bar holds open position), varStateLeakage flag (set if metaInfo declares var/varip), requestSecurityLookback bars, warmupBiasFolds[], compoundingArtifact: true.
- Output language: avoid unqualified terms 'purged CPCV', 'PBO', 'OOS distribution' in this mode. Phrase as 'approximate fold metrics' / 'contamination-flagged distribution.'

mode: exactWindowed
- Per-fold runs; each fold = independent runStrategy from bar 0 with explicit warmupBars.
- Apply purge (drop trades whose holding period straddles fold boundary) + embargo (drop first embargoBars after each test fold from any subsequent train window).
- Cost: C(N,k) x |paramGrid| x ((N-k)+1) runs.
- This is the only mode allowed to support strong claims like 'purged CPCV', 'PBO', deflated 'OOS Sharpe distribution' without qualifier.
- Response includes pbo (Probability of Backtest Overfitting per Bailey/Borwein), deflatedSharpe, distribution stats.

Both modes return mode-tagged output so downstream consumers know fidelity class. UI/docs language must reflect distinction.

Acceptance: both modes runnable; approxSlice contamination block populated correctly on a known-leaky probe (script with var state, request.security); exactWindowed PBO computed correctly on synthetic seeded random-walk strategy with no edge (PBO should approach 0.5); claim-guard wording in OpenAPI descriptions; 5 gates green.
acceptance_criteria: approxSlice mode — single full-history run per param combo; slice equity[]/trades[] into N time blocks; compute fold metrics in-memory; DEFAULT FOLD RANKING METRIC IS SORTINO (Sharpe secondary only — bd memory always-use-sortino-over-sharpe-in-metric-defaults: user does not want to reward downside volatility); response MUST include contamination block { openPositionStraddleRate, varStateLeakage flag set if metaInfo declares var/varip, requestSecurityLookback bars, warmupBiasFolds[], compoundingArtifact:true }; output language explicitly avoids unqualified terms 'purged CPCV', 'PBO', 'OOS distribution' — phrased as 'approximate fold metrics' / 'contamination-flagged distribution'. exactWindowed mode — per-fold runs from bar 0 with explicit warmupBars; purge (drop trades whose holding period straddles fold boundary) + embargo (drop first embargoBars after each test fold from any subsequent train window); cost C(N,k) × |paramGrid| × ((N-k)+1) runs; ONLY mode allowed to use 'purged CPCV' / 'PBO' / unqualified 'OOS Sortino distribution' or 'OOS Sharpe distribution' in payload field names and OpenAPI descriptions; response includes pbo (Bailey/Borwein), deflatedSortino as primary + deflatedSharpe as secondary, full distribution stats for both metrics. Default selection metric in PBO computation is Sortino. Both modes return mode-tagged output. Probes: approxSlice contamination block populated correctly on a known-leaky probe (script with var state, request.security); exactWindowed PBO computed correctly on synthetic seeded random-walk strategy with no edge (PBO approaches 0.5 under both Sortino and Sharpe selection criteria); claim-guard wording verified in OpenAPI route descriptions; 5 local gates green.

Added 'to?: number' (Unix-seconds end-of-history pin) to:

Extended packages/tradingview-core/src/analyze.ts PboDetail interface with 'trainFolds' and 'testFolds' arrays and populated them in computePbo so consumers can compute genuine test-fold OOS metrics rather than IS-winner full-history metrics.

EXACTWINDOWED REWRITE (worker/src/runners/cpcv-runner.ts)
runExactWindowed now does true per-fold isolated TV runs:
- 1 boundary-discovery run for combos[0] (bars = warmupBars + requestedTotalBars) to learn fold boundary timestamps from actual returned equity.
- foldWidth = floor((boundaryEquity.length - warmupBars) / N) (derived from actual returned length, NOT requested length, so it tolerates TV returning fewer bars than asked).
- |combos| × N per-fold cells, each one calling runStrategyCached with bars = warmupBars + (f+1)*foldWidth and to = foldEndTs[f]. KV cache short-circuits identical (combo, fold) requests across re-submissions.
- OOS distribution now uses pbo.details.testFolds to compute mean of the IS-winner's actual test-fold metrics — fixes the prior quasi-bug where it used the IS-winner's full-history metric.
- Warmup-zero warning note added.

NEW TESTS (worker/src/runners/cpcv-runner.test.ts — 14 tests total, +3 new)
- 'exactWindowed makes 1 boundary run + |combos|*N per-fold runs' — counts mock calls.
- 'exactWindowed per-fold calls pass to=foldEndTs[f] and bars=warmup+(f+1)*foldWidth' — captures req.to and req.bars on each call and asserts the boundary call has to=undefined and bars=warmup+1000, while all 8 cell calls have numeric to and bars in the expected set { warmup+1*foldWidth, ..., warmup+4*foldWidth }.
- 'exactWindowed OOS distribution uses test-fold metrics (not IS-winner full-history)' — synthesizes a known-skew (combo wins folds 0,1; loses 2,3) and asserts the OOS distribution contains negative draws (which can only happen if test-fold metrics are used; the prior IS-winner-full-history bug would have produced an all-positive OOS distribution).

title: Slice C: walkforward + matrix + analyze job runners
description:
Quant orchestration on top of BacktestJob DO. Depends on Slice B.

Scope:
- walkforward job: anchored or rolling WF over (history, isWindow, oosWindow, step, paramGrid, metric). Per-window: optimize on IS, evaluate winning params on OOS. Output: per-window winning params, OOS metrics, concatenated equity, summary stats.
- matrix job: cartesian (symbols x tfs x paramGrid x window). Concurrency cap respecting TV soft rate-limits (default 4-5 parallel WS). Output: per-cell report, ranked.
- analyze job (and synchronous /v1/strategy/analyze for small inputs): bootstrap CIs on Sharpe/Sortino/Calmar/PF/maxDD; Monte Carlo trade reorder; deflated Sharpe ratio (Lopez de Prado, accounting for trial count); permutation test; SPA / Reality Check; regime conditioning by VIX/ATR/trend bucket. Pure post-process on (trades, equity); no extra TV calls.
- KV cache hits short-circuit per-cell TV runs.

Acceptance: 3 job runners implemented; analyze suite probes against known synthetic equity (Sharpe of N(0.001, 0.01) over 1000 obs assertable); walkforward with anchored 4y IS + 1y OOS over 10y daily history runs to completion; matrix probe over 3 symbols x 2 tfs x 4 params completes; deflated Sharpe correctly accounts for trial count vs single-strategy Sharpe; 5 gates green.

title: Slice B: BacktestJob DO + job subsystem
description:
Async job orchestration for long-running backtest workloads. Depends on Slice A.

Scope:
- New DO BacktestJob (wrangler.jsonc migration tag v5, new_sqlite_classes:[BacktestJob]).
- Routes: POST /v1/jobs/submit (returns {jobId}), GET /v1/jobs/{id}/status, GET /v1/jobs/{id}/events (SSE), GET /v1/jobs/{id}/result, POST /v1/jobs/{id}/cancel.
- Idempotency: submit body includes idempotencyKey; canonical cache key = sha256 of (scriptId, scriptVersion, metaInfoVersion, resolvedInputWire including in_0, properties subset, symbol/adjustment/sessionType/chartType, endpoint, timeframe, barCount, mode chart|chartExtended|deep, useBarMagnifier, dataQuality, windowFrom/To, workerSemver). Same idempotencyKey + same canonical key returns existing jobId. Same canonical key without explicit idempotencyKey ALSO returns existing jobId by default to prevent duplicate TV workloads from retries.
- Storage split: DO SQLite owns job state (rows, queue, leases, retries); R2 owns large arrays under backtest/jobs/<jobId>/ keys; KV CACHE_META owns input-hash -> r2-key index only.
- Job types accepted in submit body: walkforward, cpcv, matrix, optimize-deep, analyze. Slice C/D implement the actual job runners.
- /v1/strategy/analyze stays synchronous for caller-supplied (trades, equity) arrays under ~1MB; large analyze workloads go through job submit.

Acceptance: DO created with migration v5; 5 routes registered; idempotency probe (submit twice with same canonical key, get same jobId, no duplicate TV calls); cancel transitions state cleanly; events SSE emits status/partial/error/done; 5 gates green.
acceptance_criteria: DO BacktestJob created with wrangler migration tag v5; 5 routes registered (POST /v1/jobs/submit, GET /v1/jobs/{id}/status, GET /v1/jobs/{id}/events SSE, GET /v1/jobs/{id}/result, POST /v1/jobs/{id}/cancel); idempotency probe — submit twice with same canonical key returns same jobId, no duplicate TV calls; canonical key sha256 covers (scriptId, scriptVersion, metaInfoVersion, resolvedInputWire incl in_0, properties subset, symbol/adjustment/sessionType/chartType, endpoint, timeframe, barCount, mode, useBarMagnifier, dataQuality, windowFrom/To, workerSemver); cancel transitions state cleanly without orphaning TV WS; events SSE emits status / partial / error / done with Last-Event-ID resume; KV CACHE_META holds index-only (input-hash → r2-key); R2 holds large arrays under backtest/jobs/<jobId>/; DO SQLite owns job rows + queue + leases + retries; 5 local gates green (typecheck, test, build, skill:validate, openapi:validate).
notes:
Slice B implementation landed and verified locally.

Files added/modified:
- worker/src/backtest-job-do.ts (new, BacktestJob DO + canonical-key helpers)
- worker/src/backtest-job-do.test.ts (new, 15 tests)
- worker/src/index.ts (5 routes, BACKTEST_JOB binding wiring, BacktestJob export)
- worker/wrangler.jsonc (migration tag v5 + BACKTEST_JOB binding)
- worker/openapi.yaml (5 paths under /v1/jobs/*)

Architecture decisions:
- Slice B ships the orchestration shell only. handleSubmit immediately transitions
  the job to terminal status="error" with code "runner_not_implemented" so callers
  see a deterministic terminal state instead of hanging in queued. Slice C/D
  replace this with real runners.
- Storage split per acceptance: DO SQLite (jobs, job_events tables) owns the
  state machine; R2 stores large result payloads under backtest/jobs/<jobId>/;
  KV CACHE_META holds canonical-key → jobId index only.
- Canonical key = sha256(sortedJsonStringify(CanonicalJobInputs)) — recursive
  key-sorted serializer ensures order-insensitive hashing across callers.
- buildJobId(canonicalKey, idempotencyKey) deterministic — same inputs always
  resolve to the same DO instance.
- SSE handler resumes via Last-Event-ID header or ?since=N query, closes
  immediately when backlog OR persisted job row is in a terminal state.

description:

UPSTREAM PROTOCOLS:
- pushstream: nginx_push_stream_module, channel-based, NOT Engine.IO. Endpoint: wss://pushstream.tradingview.com/message-pipe-ws/public. Two parallel: _pushStreamPublic (multi-channel) + _pushStreamPrivate (single 'private_<TOKEN>'). Bootstrap channel 'pushstream_set_user_channel' returns per-user channel id. SSE fallback: /message-pipe-es.
- notifications: wss://notifications.tradingview.com/news/channel
- pricealerts: HTTP /get_offline_fires already covered; live fires via pushstream private_ channel

- GET /v1/stream/alerts (SSE Accept: text/event-stream) -> pushstream private_<TOKEN> bridged. Each event: {alert_id, fire_time, value, condition}

- GET /v1/stream/notifications (SSE) -> notifications.tv

title: Review and harden Worker and TradingView skill
description: Audit worker/ and skills/tradingview for robustness gaps beyond the prior authority refactor, implement scoped fixes, verify with repo gates, and keep the Worker/core/skill authority split intact.
notes:
Audit findings (worker + skill robustness):

worker/src/index.ts
- A. HMAC signature compare at line 130 is a non-constant-time string compare; use crypto.subtle.verify so the authority gate is timing-safe.
- B. isAuthError (lines 59-67) substring-matches generic words ("expired", "unauthorized", "forbidden", "sessionid"). A non-auth error mentioning any of these falsely triggers markAuthFailure. Tighten to anchored phrases / 401|403 status codes.
- C. /cache/:symbol/:tf parses query params with Number(x) (lines 251-258); non-numeric input becomes NaN and propagates into cache logic. Validate as finite numbers and reject otherwise.

worker/src/tradingview.ts
- E. getCandles slice math bug (lines 238-240): newCandles.slice(0, -rawCandles.length) collapses to slice(0, 0) when rawCandles is empty (-0 === 0), discarding the entire first batch when TradingView returns more bars than requested. Guard on rawCandles.length > 0 or compute the cut explicitly.
- F. loginUser surfaces TradingView's raw error string into the thrown message; cap and sanitize to avoid leaking control characters / oversized payloads.

worker/src/cache.ts, prune.ts, index.ts (invalidate)
- G. KV list({ prefix }) and R2 bucket.list({ prefix }) calls do not handle cursor/list_complete or truncated pagination. After 1000 metas/chunks accumulate, snapshotMeta, pruneCache, updateTotalsAndMaybeEvict, and /cache/:symbol/:tf/invalidate silently drop the tail — orphaning R2 objects and missing eviction targets.

skills/tradingview/{SKILL.md, auth.md}
- H. SKILL.md doesn't document the partial response field, the authSource ("stored"|"provided"|"none") returned by session-aware routes, or the worker's fail-closed behavior on missing HMAC env.
- I. auth.md doesn't pin down X-Timestamp units (ms epoch), the 5-minute skew window the worker enforces, or that the same HMAC scheme covers /admin and /v1.

Out of scope this pass:
- markAuthFailure read-modify-write race (KV has no CAS; correct fix is moving auth-store behind a Durable Object).
- /v1/auth-token markAuthFailure asymmetry (would need a refactor of the route's auth-token-vs-failure heuristic).
- Engine.IO connect endpoint failure tracking across calls.
close_reason: Worker and TradingView skill robustness pass verified. Closed in-scope audit findings: HMAC verify path preserved, error classification prevents network/upstream failures from poisoning auth state, signed sessions propagate across upstream paths, cache query numbers reject non-finite values, candle batch trimming avoids -0 first-batch loss, login errors are sanitized, KV/R2 list pagination is handled, and skill/auth docs now cover partial/authSource/fail-closed/retry semantics. Verification: pnpm run typecheck, pnpm test, pnpm run build.

title: bd preflight defaults wrong for this repo (Go/Nix instead of pnpm)
description:
bd preflight prints Go/Nix checks while repo guidance requires pnpm gates per CLAUDE.md and package.json. Misleads session-close verification because the checklist does not align with the actual gate set.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. cd /Users/gwizz/CascadeProjects/Trading/tradingview
2. bd preflight
3. Observe the printed checklist references Go (go test) and/or Nix-style checks instead of pnpm.
4. Compare against the canonical gate set in CLAUDE.md and package.json scripts.

## Required gates per project (pnpm-based)
- pnpm run typecheck
- pnpm test
- pnpm run build
- pnpm run skill:validate
- pnpm run openapi:validate

## Fix options
1. Configure bd preflight via bd config to run the correct command set, OR
2. Document that bd preflight is non-authoritative for this repo in AGENTS.md and standardize on the pnpm gate list as the canonical session-close checklist, OR
3. Both.

Until fixed, do not use bd preflight as the session-close verification source. Run the 5 pnpm gates directly.
acceptance_criteria: Either bd config updated so bd preflight runs the 5 pnpm gates, or AGENTS.md explicitly documents bd preflight as non-authoritative and names the pnpm gate set as the canonical session-close checklist. Verified by running bd preflight and observing pnpm-aligned checks (option 1) OR by an AGENTS.md grep showing the override paragraph (option 2).

Option 2 from the bug description. bd config was not modified — that requires a bd repo-config change rather than a project edit.

value: StreamBridge DO (worker/src/stream-do.ts) is a per-sessionToken SSE bridge: idFromName uses the sessionToken so all SSE consumers and pollers for the same user share the same DO and ring buffer. Sub-routes are /subscribe-alerts, /subscribe-news, /sse, /poll. Index.ts forwarders use forwardToStreamBridge(c, subPath, body, sessionToken[, init]) — an init override is required for GET /sse since it needs accept:text/event-stream not POST JSON. wrangler.jsonc binding name: STREAM_BRIDGE; class: StreamBridge; migration tag v4 with new_sqlite_classes:[StreamBridge].

The downstream code requires a pineId to feed runStudy. To make source-only viable, the Worker must either (a) save the script via pine-facade/save/new_draft first to obtain pineId, or (b) feed IL+ilTemplate directly through create_study without a pineId reference. Surfaced by live probe slice-a-source-only on 2026-05-07.

TradingView edge IPs (Cloudflare Workers egress) are 403'd on www.tradingview.com/disclaimer/ and on pine-facade.tradingview.com endpoints when only Cookie is sent. Fix: include browser-fingerprint headers (User-Agent, Accept, Accept-Language, Origin, Referer, Origin where appropriate). Applied in worker/src/tradingview.ts, chart-session-do.ts, quote-stream-do.ts (disclaimer fetch) and pine.ts (cookieHeader extended to include BROWSER_HEADERS for translate_light/translate_source/eval_pine_ex). Other TV endpoints (scanner, ideas, minds, pine-facade list) have NOT been patched; surface this fix to additional sites only when probes show they're blocked.

create_study against prodata.tradingview.com WS rejects STD;<id>@tv-basicstudies-N! and Script$PUB;<hash>@tv-scripting-101! with study_error param[3]='check study unexpected error' (param[4] = backend tag like lax1-charts-pro-3-tvbs-*). Confirmed across 25+ permutations of endpoint, auth, turnaround, inputs (incl. full metaInfo defaults with the AES-encrypted text blob), namespace versions (45.0/45/241/100/99/scripting/pinescript), prefix variants (Script$, bare PUB;), and quote-session bootstrap. Series streaming continues normally after the rejection — connection is healthy. Working alternatives: legacy bare ids RSI@tv-basicstudies-45!, Volume@tv-basicstudies-118!, PivotPointsHighLow@tv-basicstudies-241!. Filed as bug tradingview-o99.

Charts-storage RS512 chart-token JWTs are minted Worker-side via mintChartToken({sessionId, sessionSign, userId, kv: CACHE_META}, layoutId, {force?: boolean}). Cache key (userId, layoutId), TTL = exp - iat - 60s. Refresh on 401/403 by invalidating cache and re-minting. Token NEVER exposed to clients — Worker uses it internally for charts-storage.tradingview.com calls. resolveChartUserId helper in worker/src/index.ts falls back to getUserProfile (tradingview.ts variant) when body.userId not provided.

TradingView create_study WebSocket wire format is 6 args: [chart_session, study_slot_id, turnaround, parent_series_id, indicator_id_with_version, inputs]. Plot output arrives in du frames keyed by study_slot_id (du.params[1][st_slot] = {t, st:[{i, v[]}], ns?}). Worker must mint sds_1 + create_series before create_study, accumulate du frames, and resolve on study_completed (or first du). Source-typed inputs wire as <seriesId>$<plot>; symbol-typed as {type:symbol,value:...}. See worker/src/tradingview.ts runStudy.

TradingView wire format hard reset (2026-05-07): three changes ride together. (1) WS endpoint MUST carry browser query string ?from=chart%2F&date=<ISO>&type=chart&auth=sessionid for any Pine create_study; bare wss://prodata/.../websocket fails with 'Study not allowed in this connection' even with valid premium cookies. (2) Built-in studies use bare id no STD; prefix no trailing ! e.g. 'EMA@tv-basicstudies-265' not 'STD;EMA@tv-basicstudies-241!'. Current pack pinned to 265. (3) Pine scripts (PUB;/USER;) ALL emit 6-arg create_study with literal wireId 'Script@tv-scripting-101!' — script identity moves into inputs dict as {text: <ilTemplate from pine-facade/translate>, pineId: 'PUB;<hash>'|'USER;<id>', pineVersion: '1.0' or fetched}. Closed-source PUB scripts have ilTemplate accessible via /translate (NOT /get which 401s). Verified end-to-end with /tmp/tv-aes-probe/script-replay-v2.mjs (proves endpoint URL gating) and algorganic-fullflow.mjs (302 du rows from Algorganic Pine v7). Constants live in packages/tradingview-core/src/constants.ts: buildChartSessionWsUrl(), TRADINGVIEW_BASICSTUDIES_VERSION='265', TRADINGVIEW_PINE_SCRIPT_WIRE_ID='Script@tv-scripting-101!'. Note: alert_info.fields.study in pine-facade gen_alert payloads still uses 'Script$PUB;<hash>@tv-scripting-101!' format — that is TV's alert-side serialization separate from create_study wireId, do not unify.

TV PUB; Pine script wireId format: literal 'Script@tv-scripting-101!' (NOT Script$PUB;<hash>@...). Script identity goes inside the inputs dict: {text: <AES-encrypted Pine IL>, pineId: 'PUB;<hash>', pineVersion: '1.0'}. Per-study options merge into the same inputs dict. Open-source PUB IL fetchable via GET https://pine-facade.tradingview.com/pine-facade/get/PUB%3B<hash>/last → returns {IL, pine_version, ...}. Closed-source PUB returns 401 'User is not allowed to see source code of pine' — likely needs WS-side fetch (request_script_text or similar). Built-in studies use bare-id format <Id>@tv-basicstudies-265 (no STD; prefix, version 265 current as of 2026-05-07, trailing ! optional).

TV WS create_study root cause: bare prodata.tradingview.com/socket.io/websocket triggers entitlement gate ('Study not allowed in this connection') for Pine-script studies (Script@tv-scripting-101!). Browser uses wss://prodata.tradingview.com/socket.io/websocket?from=chart%2F&date=<ISO>&type=chart&auth=sessionid which unlocks Pine execution. Built-in studies (Volume@tv-basicstudies-265, RSI@tv-basicstudies-265) work on either endpoint. STD;<id>@tv-basicstudies-N! is universally rejected ('check study unexpected error') on either endpoint — STD; prefix is dead. Confirmed via /tmp/tv-aes-probe/script-replay-v2.mjs.

Vitest fetchSpy.mock.calls[0] is typed as the runtime tuple ([] when no calls, full tuple after) but tsc widens to []. Direct cast 'as [string, RequestInit]' fails TS2352 (no overlap with []). Fix: 'as unknown as [string, RequestInit]' (double cast). Alternative: vi.fn<[string, RequestInit], Promise<Response>>(...) declaration. Used in worker/src/index.test.ts for chart-session DO routing tests.

scripts/validate-worker-openapi.mjs extracts routes from worker/src/index.ts using a regex that matches only literal app.METHOD("path", ...) calls, not helpers like registerXxx(path, fn) that internally call app.post. When adding bulk-route helpers, register routes explicitly with app.post("/path", (c) => helper(c, ...)) so the validator sees them. See refactor of registerAlertBulkOp -> handleAlertBulkOp + explicit app.post for /v1/alerts/{delete,stop,restart,clone}.
Always prefer pnpm over npm in this project (and the user prefers pnpm globally). Use pnpm install, pnpm test, pnpm run <script>, pnpm-workspace.yaml for workspaces, pnpm/action-setup in CI.

Pine compile diagnostic normalization: when TradingView pine-facade responses have no 'result' wrapper, 'const result = data?.result ?? data' makes result === data. Calling collectDiagnostics(data, result) then double-counts errors/warnings/reason/reason2. Fix: dedup by reference inside collectDiagnostics with a 'seen: Set<any>' guard so each source object is processed at most once. See worker/src/pine.ts.

worker/src/index.ts: requirePineCtx(c, body, { writeOp?: boolean }) returns either { error: Response } (when writeOp && no session) or { session, ctx }. ALL callers — even read-only ones with writeOp:false — must do 'if ("error" in r) return r.error;' before accessing r.ctx/r.session, otherwise tsc widens the union and r.ctx becomes undefined. The helper's TypeScript narrowing is conservative because writeOp is a runtime flag, not a type-level discriminator. Same applies to requireTemplateCtx and requireWatchlistCtx.

Always use Sortino over Sharpe in metric defaults — user explicitly does not want to reward downside volatility. Apply to: Slice C analyze suite default ranking metric, Slice D CPCV distribution metric, matrix runner default ranking metric, walkforward winning-params default metric. Sharpe may appear as a secondary comparison metric but must not be the default ranking criterion.

Cloudflare Workers Durable Object migrations in wrangler.jsonc: every new SQLite-backed DO class needs its own migration tag with new_sqlite_classes:[name]. To delete a class use a separate tag with deleted_classes:[name]. Adding ChartSession used tag v3 with new_sqlite_classes:[ChartSession]. Pre-existing v2 has deleted_classes:[StreamHub] which fails 'cf-typegen' if StreamHub class never existed in production state — typegen requires the deleted class to currently exist; this pre-dates worktree work and does not block typecheck/test/build/openapi/skill gates.

pricealerts.tradingview.com requires {payload:<obj>} body wrap on POST routes, query string with log_username + maintenance_unset_reason on every call (build_time on POST, user_id on GET /list_alerts). Response envelope is {s:"ok"|"error", id?, r?}; readEnvelope must throw on s=error. /is_alive accepts plain text (true/OK). alerts.tradingview.com/alerts/health/ is legacy healthcheck only. See worker/src/alerts.ts.

TV pine-facade /pine-facade/versions/<id>/last returns floating-point versions (e.g. STD;RSI=45.0, STD;EMA=36.0, STD;Volume=30.0) but the worker tradingview.ts:resolveStudyWireId concatenates raw which produces tv-basicstudies-45.0 — this concatenation is invalid AND irrelevant: the underlying STD;<id>@<anything>! family is fully rejected by TV's WS regardless of whether you use 45, 45.0, last, or any other suffix. Confirmed via /tmp/tv-aes-probe/wireid-namespace-matrix.mjs.

TradingView modify_study WebSocket wire format is 5 args: [chart_session, study_slot_id, turnaround, indicator_id_with_version, inputs]. Note no parent_series_id (vs 6-arg create_study). Used to update inputs on an existing study without re-creating it; same du frame keyed by study_slot_id. See worker/src/study-chain.ts modifyStudy and chart-session DO /study/modify handler.

Worker /v1/jobs/submit hit a Cloudflare DO sqlite cursor.one() runtime divergence: when the WHERE id=? row count is zero, .one() throws on the live runtime but the test fake returns null. Probe surfaced this as 500 status with success-shape body. Fixed by switching to .toArray()[0] ?? null in worker/src/backtest-job-do.ts (handleSubmit lookup + singleJob). Future work: harmonize the test fake's .one() to match runtime semantics so this divergence is caught in CI.
